rationale: Multi-distribution mixture — the direct path to the Goals bar (competitive on ≥2 of POPE/MMBench/RealWorldQA with ONE student). Proven bidirectionally that the student clears exactly the benchmark matching its training distribution (COCO→POPE 68.3, ScienceQA→MMBench 0.65). The ScienceQA pilot collapsed POPE because ScienceQA dominated (77%) with weak 0.3 rehearsal. Fix: BALANCED 50/50 mix — grounding primary (qa_balanced_5k, 1386) + scienceqa_mcq replayed at frac 1.0 (adds 1386) = 2772 rows, 50/50, 3 epochs so each skill gets ~3 passes. LoRA rank held at 16 to isolate the mixture variable vs the single-skill bests. Hypothesis: one student clears BOTH POPE and MMBench floors. If it trades skills, the next lever is capacity (rank/model size), not data.

report.note: built; run runners/eval_student.py for same-path scores.
